commands.review_summary:
The PR blocks public exact-version skill/package metadata and scan responses when the requested skill version is the moderated source version, adds regression tests, and records the moderation invariant in the security spec.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main has no source-version-aware block in the shared access helper, and the scan route reports moderation against the latest version rather than the persisted moderation source version. I did not execute tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A human maintainer should review the draft and approve the intended API compatibility/security-boundary change; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: fix: block moderated skill version metadata
target.body:
## Summary
- block exact skill-version metadata and scan responses when the requested version is the moderated source version
- apply the same source-version moderation guard to package-compatible skill version metadata
- cover hidden public-null scan/version fallbacks, source-version scan reporting, and pending moderation reason variants

## Verification
- `bun run test convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts`
- `bun run format:check -- convex/lib/skillFileAccess.ts convex/skills.ts convex/httpApiV1/skillsV1.ts convex/httpApiV1/packagesV1.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts specs/security-moderation.md`
- `git diff --check`
- final autoreview: no findings
- `bun run ci:static`
- `bun run ci:unit`
- `bun run ci:types-build`
target.author: vincentkoc
